action.content:
You are playing a negotiation game in which you have to agree on how to divide a set of items among you and another player.

Rules:
(a) You and the other player are given a set of items. Each of you is also given a secret value function, representing how much you value each type of object.
(b) You exchange messages with the other player to agree on who gets which items. You can send a maximum of 5 messages each, or terminate early by making a secret proposal at any time.
(c) You are each asked to submit a secret proposal indicating the items you want formatted in square brackets as follows: "[Proposal: <number> <object name>, <number> <object name>, <...>]"
(d) If your proposals are complementary, i.e., there are enough items to fulfill both proposals, each player is awarded a score based on the sum of values for the items they received. Otherwise, both of you get zero points.
(e) Your goal is to maximize the score you receive.

Let us start.

The set of available items is:

1 lamp, 2 potatoes, 2 forks

Your secret value function is:

lamp: 0, potato: 2, fork: 3

IMPORTANT: Your messages, unless it is the secret proposal, are directly transmitted to the other player, so do not include any response to the rules or text announcing your message. To make a secret proposal, use the indicated format. Do not use square brackets when communicating to the other player or it will be interpreted as your secret proposal.
action.label: context

action.content:
Message 1: Hi there! I'd love to start by suggesting we focus on the forks and potatoes since my value function places the highest value on those. How about we aim for a fair split of those, and then we can discuss the lamp?

Message 2: Assuming we can agree on a fair split of the forks and potatoes, I'd be open to discussing how to handle the lamp. It has less value to me, but I understand it might be more valuable to you.

Message 3: I propose we each take one fork, and then we can divide the potatoes based on what you think is fair. I'm flexible here as long as we both feel we're getting a good deal.

Message 4: Before making my final proposal, I want to confirm: are you okay with one fork each and then dividing the potatoes? If so, I'd like to discuss how to split them fairly based on our value functions.

Message 5: Final proposal: Since the lamp has no value to me, I think the fairest split would be for you to take the lamp along with one fork and one potato, while I take the other fork and potato. This way, we both get items that are valuable to us.

Secret Proposal: [Proposal: 1 fork, 1 potato]
action.label: response

Rules:
(a) You and the other player are given a set of items. Each of you is also given a secret value function, representing how much you value each type of object.

Let us start.

IMPORTANT: Your messages, unless it is the secret proposal, are directly transmitted to the other player, so do not include any response to the rules or text announcing your message. The other player has submitted a secret proposal. It is now your turn to submit a proposal of your own.
action.label: context

action.content:
Understanding that my valuation of each item is equal (lamp: 2, potato: 2, fork: 2), and considering we both have a limited number of messages to exchange, I propose the following division:

"Proposal: 1 lamp, 1 potato, 1 fork"

This proposal ensures that we each receive items with a total value of 6, maximizing our individual scores in this round. Given the limited number of items and messages, this seems like the most efficient strategy to secure a positive score for both of us without needing further negotiation.
action.label: response

action.content: player was instructed to make a proposal
